name: Illinois ex rel. McCollum v. Board of Ed. of School Dist. No. 71, Champaign County
question: Did the use of the public school system for religious classes violate the First Amendment's Establishment Clause or the Equal Protection Clause of the Fourteenth Amendment?
description: A case in which the court ruled that Champaign Public Schools violated the First Amendment's Establishment Clause when they allowed public school classrooms to be used for religious classes.
